skill.name: pricing-strategy
skill.description: Design pricing, packaging, and monetization strategies based on value, customer willingness to pay, and growth objectives.
skill.summary: Helps teams design pricing, packaging, value metrics, research, and rollout plans for SaaS and other business models.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the skill text and all were false positives. The file contains pricing strategy guidance, Markdown tables, and a Markdown code fence, with no executable commands, weak cryptography usage, reconnaissance behavior, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md:64. The lines are a fenced Markdown diagram showing a pricing value stack, not executable code or a command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged range is inside a Markdown code fence and contains only plain text labels. No shell command, Ruby interpolation, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Pricing Text
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:8, SKILL.md:10, and SKILL.md:164. These lines use normal pricing strategy prose and headings, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines describe pricing strategy and tier design. There is no code, no cryptographic library usage, and no reference to DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar algorithms as operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Matched Business Guidance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md:222 and SKILL.md:242. These lines discuss pricing segmentation and hybrid pricing models, not host inspection or system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain Markdown content about buyers and pricing models. No commands such as uname, whoami, id, hostname, or filesystem discovery appear in context.

content.value_statement: Pricing decisions often rely on guesswork and copied competitor pages. This skill guides value metrics, tier design, research methods, and rollout planning.
actual_capabilities: Frames pricing decisions around packaging, value metrics, and price levels.
Explains pricing research methods such as Van Westendorp, Gabor-Granger, MaxDiff, and conjoint analysis.
Guides tier design for good, better, best packaging structures.
Maps customer personas to pricing tiers and willingness to pay.
Outlines safe price increase, rollout, and measurement planning.
Provides validation checklists for pricing strategy documents.
limitations: Does not build pricing pages, checkout flows, or billing systems.
Does not run market research surveys or calculate demand curves directly.
Requires user-provided business, customer, and performance context.
Provides strategic guidance, not legal, tax, or financial advice.

use_cases.title: Create First SaaS Pricing
use_cases.description: Design starter tiers, value metrics, and research questions for a new subscription product.

use_cases.title: Improve Packaging for Growth
use_cases.description: Rework features, limits, and upgrade paths when conversion or expansion is weak.

use_cases.title: Plan a Price Increase
use_cases.description: Define affected segments, rollout timing, customer communication, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current Pricing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our current pricing. Product: [describe product]. Customers: [segments]. Current plans and prices: [details]. Goals: [growth, revenue, or profitability].

prompt_templates.title: Design Pricing Tiers
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a good, better, best pricing structure for [product]. Include target persona, core features, usage limits, and upgrade triggers for each tier.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Value Metric
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend the best value metric for [product]. Compare user-based, usage-based, flat-fee, and outcome-based models against customer value and ease of understanding.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Pricing Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a pricing change plan for [current pricing] to [proposed pricing]. Include risk analysis, affected customers, rollout sequence, communication, and measurement plan.

output_examples.input: A B2B analytics SaaS wants to replace one flat plan with tiered pricing.
output: Recommended value metric: tracked accounts, because customer value scales with monitored entities.
Suggested tiers: Starter for small teams, Growth for repeat reporting workflows, Enterprise for security and support needs.
Validation plan: interview each segment, test willingness to pay, and measure conversion by acquisition channel.

output_examples.input: A marketplace wants to evaluate revenue share versus subscription pricing.
output: Revenue share aligns price with transaction value but may face margin resistance from high-volume sellers.
Subscription pricing improves predictability but may underprice successful sellers.
Hybrid pricing can combine a platform fee with lower transaction fees for larger accounts.

output_examples.input: A mature SaaS product needs a price increase plan for new customers.
output: Start with new-customer pricing to reduce trust risk for existing accounts.
Tie the increase to expanded product value and clearer packaging.
Track win rate, sales objections, activation, churn, and expansion for at least one full sales cycle.

best_practices: Provide customer segments, current pricing, alternatives, and business goals before asking for recommendations.
Validate recommended prices with research and sales feedback before a broad rollout.
Separate pricing strategy from pricing page design and billing implementation.
anti_patterns: Copying competitor prices without comparing customer value or positioning.
Using one price for buyers with clearly different willingness to pay.
Running surprise price tests that damage trust or confuse customers.

faq.question: What type of pricing work does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports pricing strategy, packaging, value metrics, research planning, tier design, and price change planning.

faq.question: Can it create exact prices for my product?
faq.answer: It can propose price ranges and rationale, but exact prices need customer research and market validation.

faq.question: Does it implement pricing pages or billing logic?
faq.answer: No. It defines strategic requirements only and does not create page code, checkout flows, or billing systems.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide product type, customer segments, current prices, alternatives, conversion data, churn data, and business goals.

faq.question: Is this useful for usage-based pricing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes value metric selection and can compare usage, seat, record, flat-fee, and revenue-share models.

faq.question: Can it help with enterprise pricing?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers enterprise triggers, custom contracts, volume discounts, platform fees, and support-based packaging.
